{{Quote|C'mon, get up!|Oberk to Leia Organa|<ref name="Episode VI" />}}
'''Oberk''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout trooper]] and a [[Corporal/Legends|corporal]] at the time of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|What the-?|Oberk shortly before losing consciousness|<ref name="Episode VI" />}}
Oberk was originally from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ukio/Legends|Ukio]].<ref name="CCG Oberk" /> He at some point prior to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] joined the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]].<ref name="Episode VI" /> By 4 ABY he gained the rank of [[Corporal/Legends|Corporal]]<ref name="CCG Oberk" /> and was stationed on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] to help guard the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star's]] [[Endor shield generator bunker|shield generator bunker]] and patrol the moon's forests.<ref name="Episode VI" /> While on Endor, he tried to impress his biker scout detachment commander.<ref name="CCG Oberk" />

While patrolling Endor's forests with his partner, Oberk noticed [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] operative [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. Her [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] companion, [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] realized they were being watched but was unable to warn Organa in time.<ref name="CCG Freeze">{{CCG|set=Endor Limited|cardname=Freeze!}}</ref> Oberk's partner fired two shots at Organa, causing her to take cover and distracting her long enough for Oberk to sneak up beside her and order her to freeze. While Oberk held her at [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster]]-point, he ordered his comrade to ready his speeder, but Wicket, who had hid under a log, attacked Oberk with his [[Spear/Legends|spear]]. Oberk was momentarily distracted before Organa bludgeoned him with a branch and knocked him unconscious.<ref name="Episode VI" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Oberk first appeared as a scout trooper extra in the film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], released in [[1983]]. In the film, he was portrayed by an uncredited actor. Prior to the film, in the [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Return of the Jedi'' novel]] released earlier that same year, an unnamed scout trooper with Oberk's role appeared. He is alone when capturing Leia Organa, has different dialogue in the scene, and Organa kills him with his own blaster after Wicket Warrick attacks him. In the same year as the film and the novel, Oberk was also pictured in the ''[[Return of the Jedi: The Storybook Based on the Movie]]'', although the text is similar to the novel in describing a single scout trooper attempting to capture Organa, and being killed by her with Warrick's assistance.<ref name="Storybook">''[[Return of the Jedi: The Storybook Based on the Movie]]''</ref> This article avoids treating the novel as a confirmed Oberk appearance, and lists the ''Return of the Jedi Storybook'' as an appearance only because it pictures the character.

In [[1999]], Oberk was given a name and a brief backstory in the [[Endor Limited]] edition of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game]].
